findings.title: Native-ETH withdrawals are broken for non-WETH pairs

best_agent_match.title: Native-ETH withdrawals are broken for non-WETH pairs

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Validation for Identical Tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: `deposit()` and `withdraw()` require `factory.getPair(token0, token1) != address(0)`, so identical-token inputs are rejected unless the factory itself exposes such a pair.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Integer Overflow in `_calculateSwapAmount`
rejected_candidates.reason: Solidity 0.8 reverts on arithmetic overflow, and realistic reserve/input sizes are far below the range needed to make this a practical protocol issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Operators Can Withdraw from Any User's Basket
rejected_candidates.reason: The nonzero-`basketId` branch only lets operators force a withdrawal back to the same `account`; it does not redirect funds. Any operator theft is already more directly covered by `withdrawToken`.

rejected_candidates.title: No Deadline Check on Router Interactions
rejected_candidates.reason: Passing `block.timestamp` as the router deadline forces same-block execution rather than allowing indefinite pending execution, so the stated issue is backwards.

rejected_candidates.title: OperatorSetter Role is Irreversible
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a governance/trust-model concern, not a standalone exploitable vulnerability in the contract logic.

rejected_candidates.title: Balance Check Before `transferFrom` Allows Token Manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: The before/after balance pattern is a standard way to support fee-on-transfer tokens here, and no concrete reentrant accounting exploit is supported by the contract state transitions.

rejected_candidates.title: Refund Calculation Can Underflow
rejected_candidates.reason: `addLiquidity()` returns the amounts actually consumed, which are bounded by the desired amounts; the subtraction only reverts if the router breaks its interface guarantees.

rejected_candidates.title: Unrestricted ETH Receive Function
rejected_candidates.reason: Accepting unsolicited ETH may create recoverable dust, but by itself it does not create realistic protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Return Value Check on Reward Claim
rejected_candidates.reason: `IRewarderv2.claim()` is declared with no return value, so there is no unchecked boolean or amount to validate.
